validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_self_test_fixture -v passes 11/11 (0.002s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 331/332 with 1 OS-specific skip (38.3s, up from 321 -- 11 new cases for unit_054)

checkpoint.summary: unit_054 complete. Self-test fixture scaffolded at skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/fixtures/self-test/ with a 2-phase / 6-unit mini-CRUD roadmap ('Tasklet' -- Items + Users resources). ROADMAP.md describes the product intent + the 'this is a harness self-test fixture' framing. README.md is the binding contract between unit_054 and downstream units 055-058: directory-contents table with per-artifact creating-unit attribution, ASCII phase graph showing all 6 units with exact touches_paths declarations, 3 seeded conditions documented with specific unit ids + the exact downstream-observable strings operators will see (path_overlap_with:unit_a2, src/seeds/users.json, conflict.category: 'scope_violation'), Expected end state section with orphan-free invariants, operator recipe. Seeded conditions: (1) Batch >= 2 via unit_a1 + unit_a2 disjoint touches_paths in PHASE_A; (2) Overlap-matrix rejection via unit_a3 declaring src/items/routes.py which also appears in unit_a2's touches_paths; (3) Scope violation via unit_b2's description requiring also writing src/seeds/users.json which is outside its declared touches_paths. New test_self_test_fixture.py (140 LOC, 11 cases across 5 classes) pins fixture presence + roadmap shape + all 3 seeded conditions with their downstream-observable strings + forward-references to the 4 planned artifacts + expected-end-state invariants + a regression guard that fixtures don't accidentally contain test_*.py modules. Suite 321 -> 332.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_055 (PHASE_013): run /create-development-harness on the self-test fixture. The fixture has ROADMAP.md but no .harness/ yet. Running /create walks Phases 0-7, populates touches_paths per unit as laid out in README.md's ASCII phase graph, sets config.execution_mode.parallelism.enabled=true (matches the 'y' answer for the Phase 2 Execution Mode question from unit_048), captures the resulting phase-graph.json + config.json into the fixture directory as part of the fixture's committed state for downstream unit_056 to invoke against. Validation: manual inspection + grep for expected fields (parallelism.enabled=true, touches_paths populated on all 6 units matching README.md's graph, two phases with the expected unit ids a1/a2/a3/b1/b2/b3).
